title: Any way to ALWAYS retrieve from manifest.googlevideo.com instead of rr#.googlevideo.com using proto:mpd?
author: HomeStarRunnerTron
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I've read the [**NO AI / NO LLM POLICY**](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#no-ai--no-llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

Hi! Thank you so much to everyone who's contributed to this incredible project. Last month, I started using yt-dlp to grab m3u8s from YouTube to stream through an external app. I was grabbing the m3u8s through the "-gS proto:mpd" command, which was already a little luck-of-the-draw. It'd fluctuate pretty often between grabbing rr1/rr2 (and other googlevideo subdomains I'm probably forgetting) links and manifest.googlevideo.com links... now it seems like it's once-in-a-blue-moon. All the times I've tried to generate the right link today, I've only successfully gotten "manifest.googlevideo.com" once today. Is there something I'm doing wrong on my side-- some runtime I should be using? (I'm using deno, it appears to be grabbing from Android VR Player, etc...) The failed attempt is included in the Complete Verbose Information.

title: Spectrum MSO Unable to Extract RelayState
author: rando2024
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I've read the [**NO AI / NO LLM POLICY**](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#no-ai--no-llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I'm reporting a bug unrelated to a specific site
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar issues **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

I'm not sure it this is the right category to post this, but as a follow-up to #16983, there appears to be a broader issue with logins using the Spectrum MSO getting hung up about being unable to extract RelayState.  #16983 documents the problem when trying to access the History channel, or using the [aenetworks] extractor in general.  I've started running into the problem with the [TBS] extractor now, too (see below).  Since it looks like this is a growing issue, I decided to file a separate bug report about this issue.  Thanks.

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)

title: Support for the highest-quality tokcdn.com source
author: moon96m
body:
Hello everyone,

Some services return higher-quality TikTok videos through working tokcdn.com links, such as:

https://v16.tokcdn.com/ccf07d1e490f34acf09eefb9497c46db/651dfc80/7286507734655061281_original.mp4

For this TikTok video:

https://vt.tiktok.com/ZSXjHUWbe/

the currently extracted file is under 6 MB and has a relatively low bitrate, while the same video is available through other services at around 16 MB with a significantly higher bitrate.

Why is the higher-bitrate original source not currently extracted? Has anyone found how these services obtain or generate the original tokcdn.com links?

Thank you

title: Non-dowloaded subtitle options showing in mpv
author: BitSynKed
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I've read the [**NO AI / NO LLM POLICY**](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#no-ai--no-llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

Hello, I'm fairly new to trying to configure yt-dlp, so I assume my issue is just me not doing something right. Though I've also already found a workaround. I'm trying to watch youtube videos via mpv with subtitles. yt-dlp and mpv are installed via homebrew on my M1 macbook air with macos 26.5.2, with this section in my yt-dlp.config:
```
#Subtitles
--write-subs
--write-auto-subs
--sub-langs "en.*"
--embed-subs
```

`yt-dlp -s https://youtu.be/M9m7ddMatZg` shows that only english subs are being downloaded as they should. And when playing in mpv it successfully embeds the english subs. The problem is that the subtitles list in mpv shows all 150+ subtitle options regardless of whether they were downloaded. Clicking a non-english sub doesn't show anything, so its really just about cluttering up the list. 

The way I was able to get it to remove the clutter is by adding `ytdl-raw-options=sub-langs="en.*"` to my mpv.config. With this mpv's subtitle list only shows the actually downloaded english subs. 

So I'm really just trying to figure out why passing the same argument via raw-options has one behavior but the same argument in the config file gives me a different behavior.

- [x] Run **your** yt-dlp command with **-vU** flag added (`yt-dlp -vU <your command line>`)

### Region

China

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

MangoTV (in China) also provides a kind of URL which looks like this:  
https://www.mgtv.com/s/3186853.html

It points to the same video as https://www.mgtv.com/b/100994/3186780.html.

However, yt-dlp doesn't support the `/s/` type video URLs currently, and when I tried `yt-dlp https://www.mgtv.com/s/3186853.html`, it will just fallback to generic extractor and fail; while `yt-dlp https://www.mgtv.com/b/100994/3186780.html` worked just fine.

Even though the `/s/` type video URLs might be too rare to be seen (and it doesn't work on the international version, `w.mgtv.com`), I hope that could be patched.

### Provide a description that is worded well enough to be understood

Sometimes YouTube seems to swap formats mid-stream. What I've seen very often is a stream starting with format 248 (vp9 1080p30) and then, after ~10 minutes, this format disappears and is replaced with 303 (vp9 1080p60).

When this happens, the DASH downloader ends up in a broken state: the video stream errors out, but it still keeps downloading the audio stream instead of stopping the whole download process and proceeding to postprocessing stage.

I have tried with SABR too, and the format swap still happens on YouTube side but SABR seems to handle this correctly - the full download stops and goes to postprocessing instead of continuing downloading just the audio.

I don't have verbose logs from yt-dlp directly because it's a bit hard to catch so I attached some logs from a program where I'm using yt-dlp's Python API, hopefully it's enough to understand the problem I'm describing. I will try to get actual verbose logs if I can catch it next time.

title: hotaudio.net: is a browser-based extractor plugin even architecturally viable? (re #10343)
author: CaptainChicky
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I've read the [**NO AI / NO LLM POLICY**](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#no-ai--no-llm-policy) and understand I may be blocked from the repository if it is violated
- [x] I'm asking a question and **not** reporting a bug or requesting a feature
- [x] I've looked through the [README](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#readme)
- [x] I've verified that I have **updated yt-dlp to nightly or master** ([update instructions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp#update-channels))
- [x] I've searched [known issues](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3766), [the FAQ](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/FAQ), and the [bugtracker](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20-label%3Aspam%20%20) for similar questions **including closed ones**. DO NOT post duplicates

### Please make sure the question is worded well enough to be understood

I know #10343 was closed as "wontfix" with a DRM label, but I want to ask a narrower question about whether a plugin approach is viable here, since @bashonly mentioned that direction before locking. Previous attempts to support hotaudio (including the older versions of [medy17/hotaudio-downloader](https://github.com/medy17/hotaudio-downloader), now archived) tried to reverse-engineer the site's specific cipher (ChaCha20-Poly1305). These broke quickly because the site dev actively monitors for and patches against downloaders. The #10343 thread was closed partly on those grounds, since maintaining a site-specific decryption extractor that breaks every few days isn't viable in mainline, and I agree with that.

However, I have a working implementation that sidesteps the cipher entirely. Instead of reversing the encryption, we simply hook a browser's MediaSource API at the SourceBuffer.appendBuffer() level, capturing audio after the site's own JS has already decrypted it. This method doesn't care about the encryption algorithm, and bypasses the anti-tamper evasion in a way that is unpatchable by the site dev. We can also extract out any metadata from the DOM.

The problem is that the POC I have requires Playwright driving a real Chromium instance as the decryption oracle. This has a few issues that I feel are at odds with yt-dlp's design (heavy dependency with playwright+chromium, and the download time being audio time/2 + some arbitrary buffer).

Hence I have a few questions:
1. Does the yt-dlp plugin API work with this? My POC launches a browser, plays audio in real-time, and captures the decrypted output in a blob. Is this fine for a plugin, or would this need to be structured differently (postprocessor, standalone tool that outputs yt-dlp-compatible metadata, &c.)?
2. Is a playwright dependency acceptable for a plugin? I know phantomJS was used in youtube-dl historically, but playwright seems quite large even compared to phantom so idk.

Also I feel like I should mention that regarding the "DRM" label, I understand the pragmatic reasons it was applied, but for the record, this is a **JS-based encryption scheme** with browser-side key material, **NOT** a hardware-backed DRM (Widevine/PlayReady/FairPlay). This can't be considered a DRM, as there's no CDM, no license server handshake, no TEE, and hence is trivially bypassable via the strat I mentioned above. I just wanted to note this in case the DRM label would be something of concern. 

I'd be happy to share my implementation if anyone wants to evaluate feasibility, but anyhow I just wanted to put this out here since even though the download method here is foolproof, idk how it would mesh with yt-dlp.

- [ ] If using API, add `'verbose': True` to `YoutubeDL` params instead
